channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: France and Morocco Clash in World Cup Quarter-Final as Mbappe's Penalty Saved
item.description: France face their most significant challenge yet at the World Cup as they take on Morocco in a repeat of the 2022 semi-final. Didier Deschamps' squad, led by Kylian Mbappe, has dominated the tournamen...

item.title: Akira Kurosawa's 'Ran' explores family succession and chaos in feudal Japan
item.description: The article discusses Akira Kurosawa's 1985 film 'Ran', a Shakespearean adaptation set in 16th-century Japan. It delves into the story of a noble family's succession crisis, which spirals into violenc...

item.title: Key Philippine and Global Headlines for July 9, 2026
item.description: The article highlights the most significant news stories from July 9, 2026. Key points include Vice President Sara Duterte's chief of staff, Zuleika Lopez, making a surprise appearance at the Senate, ...

item.title: Anthony Albanese's apology highlights media fragmentation amid political podcast culture
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faced backlash after making an inappropriate joke about Kylie Minogue during a podcast game, sparking discussions about the challenges of maintaining decorum in public ...

item.title: Pastor’s Wife Faces Trial Delay After Co-Accused Pleads Guilty in R4.1m Fraud Case
item.description: Lingasperi 'Leigh' Madurai, a former human resources manager at P&A Fabricators, faces fraud charges after her co-accused, former creditors clerk Senamile Mthabela, pleaded guilty to receiving R257,32...

item.title: IGP Calls for Global Tech Collaboration in Policing While Stressing Importance of Public Trust

item.title: France and Morocco clash in FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final
item.description: France and Morocco commenced their FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final clash in Boston, with both teams showcasing tactical discipline and resilience. The match, which began at 6:03am, saw early chances...

item.title: Economist Suggests Reforming Equalization Payments to Reduce Provincial Tensions

item.title: Nigeria halts third-party visa services for US applicants
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has suspended Online Integrated Solutions (OIS) services, which previously handled visa applications for US citizens. Applicants now must submit their application...

item.title: Angus Taylor criticizes One Nation's fiscal risks and warns of economic turmoil
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has launched a scathing critique of the One Nation party, warning that if it were to form government, it would plunge Australia into an 'eternity of pain' due to its rec...

item.title: England dominate India with 9-wicket victory to clinch T20I series
item.description: In a thrilling encounter at Bristol, England secured a decisive nine-wicket victory over India in the second T20I of the series. Harry Brook's composed innings of 79 and Phil Salt's explosive 59 off 4...

item.title: Naira depreciates amid seasonal dollar demand for travel and school fees
item.description: The Nigerian naira is experiencing renewed depreciation in the parallel foreign exchange market due to increased demand for dollars driven by summer travel, overseas school fees, and business trips. T...

item.title: The Last Dinner Party release new single ‘Knocking At The Sky’ from ‘From The Pyre’ deluxe edition

item.title: Zambia Vice President Survives Helicopter Crash During Campaign Tour
item.description: Zambia's vice president, Mutale Nalumango, survived a military helicopter crash that occurred minutes after take-off during a campaign tour in Nakonde. The incident happened as she was traveling with ...

item.title: European Parliament Reinstates Tech Companies' Right to Scan Private Messages for Child Abuse Detection
item.description: The European Parliament has voted to extend legislation allowing tech companies like Meta, Google, and Microsoft to scan users’ private messages for child sexual abuse material under the 'Chat Control...

item.title: stutter-step penalty technique in football: a double-edged sword for top players
item.description: the article explores the stutter-step penalty technique in football, a method where players use a rhythmic, exaggerated run-up to deceive goalkeepers. originated in the 1980s by players like pele and ...

item.title: Building Resilient Businesses: Strategies for Economic Uncertainty
